name: Sales copilot suite
description: Seven specialist agents that coach a sales rep in real time. Demonstrates turn-based, keyword, and event (Phase-2) triggers, fact/queue/variable/memory writes, priority, and cooldown gating.

agents.name: Phase Tracker
agents.text: You silently track which phase of the sales call we are in.
agents._doc: Silent state agent. Writes the `phase` variable every turn. Never whispers. Watch the Blackboard panel: `phase` flips discovery -> negotiation -> closing.

agents.name: Question Detector
agents.text: Detect when the customer asks a question and signal other agents.
agents._doc: Emits the `question_detected` EVENT and queues the question. The event wakes the Answer Suggester in Phase 2 of the SAME turn -- this is the framework's one-hop coordination.

agents.name: Answer Suggester
agents.text: When a question is detected, suggest how to answer it.
agents._doc: EVENT-triggered. Runs ONLY in Phase 2, and ONLY on turns where Question Detector emitted `question_detected`. On every other turn it shows up under 'skipped (trigger_type_mismatch)'. This is the clearest view of Phase-1 -> Phase-2 coordination and the +1 LLM round-trip it costs.

speak.content: Customer asked a question. Answer it directly first, then bridge to value before they disengage.

agents.name: Objection Handler
agents.text: Spot price objections and coach the rep to defend value.
agents._doc: Fires on price-objection language. Cooldown is 10s on purpose: when two objections land <10s apart, the second is gated by cooldown (see the 'skipped' list). Tracks objection_count in private memory and records an `objection` fact.

speak.content: Price objection. Anchor on ROI and total value -- do not lead with a discount.

agents.name: Buying Signal Detector
agents.text: Detect buying signals and prompt the rep to advance the deal.
agents._doc: Turn-based opportunity spotter. Fires on intent-to-buy language and nudges the rep to propose next steps.

speak.content: Strong buying signal. Propose concrete next steps and a timeline now.

agents.name: Budget Extractor
agents.text: Extract any budget figure the customer mentions as a structured fact.
agents._doc: Silent fact extractor. Matches money patterns and writes a `budget` fact to the Blackboard (no whisper). Watch the Facts section of the Blackboard panel.

agents.name: Compliance Monitor
agents.text: Catch risky absolute claims and over-promises by the rep.
agents._doc: Highest priority (10). Fires when anyone uses absolute/guarantee language so the rep doesn't over-commit. Priority decides who wins on colliding writes -- compliance should win.

speak.content: Compliance risk: avoid absolute guarantees. Use 'typically' or 'in most cases' instead.
